prompt: A dramatic Chinese ink-wash fantasy portrait based on the single Chinese character 嗔, showing a male xianxia or wuxia warrior from the back in three-quarter profile, turning slightly to the side. He has long, windswept black hair tied in a high half-up topknot with an ornate dark metallic hair crown, with several thin red ribbons threaded through the hair and trailing in the wind. His clothing is a black embroidered robe with subtle gold patterning on the shoulder and collar, layered and heavy, with flowing fabric merging into the surrounding brushwork. The face is mostly obscured by angle and hair, creating a mysterious and intense mood. The overall composition is vertical poster art with a pale parchment background, textured with gray and black ink splashes, a large faded circular brushstroke halo behind the figure’s head, and violent red paint streaks sweeping across the lower half and right side like blood-red calligraphy in motion. Use a limited palette of black, charcoal gray, parchment beige, and deep crimson red. Add 2 calligraphic text areas: a vertical poem column in black Chinese calligraphy on the upper left with a small red seal stamp beneath it, and a very large bold red Chinese character 嗔 painted at the lower left. Beside the large red character, place a vertical Romanized label reading CHEN. The style should feel like expressive ink painting mixed with ultra-detailed cinematic character illustration, with flying hair strands, splattered brush textures, emotional tension, and a dark, wrathful, elegant atmosphere.
